literal: other-extending, dependent on another; from para 'other' + tantra 'extension, system'
invariant_definition: Dependent — possessing existence and activity derived from a *svatantra* source. In Madhva's ontology, *paratantra* names the entire ontological field outside Viṣṇu — including Lakṣmī (the highest *paratantra*), all *jīvas* in the *taratamya*-graded hierarchy, *prakṛti*, *kāla*, *karma*, the Vedas (in some uses), and all of inert matter. *Paratantra* is real; it is not modal or illusory. This is structurally distinct from both Spinozistic mode-doctrine and Christian creationism.

per_school.definition: *Paratantra-tattva* is everything except Viṣṇu, and is graded internally by *taratamya*. Lakṣmī, then Brahmā and Vāyu, then the senior gods, then the senior *jīvas*, on down. The dependence is real: every *paratantra* requires Viṣṇu's continuous willing for its existence and activity. But this dependence does not erase the *paratantra*'s own real existence and own real activity (against Advaita).

translator_note: 'Dependent' captures the essential. Note: *paratantra* in classical Madhva does NOT mean 'derivative' in the Spinozistic-modal sense; it is real-with-derived-existence.
